rationale: The pair is likely substantive because both papers focus on anchoring effects in consequential pricing/payment contexts: the citing paper examines anchoring in real pay-what-you-want transactions, while the cited paper shows incidental prices shift willingness to pay in field, lab auction, and archival auction settings. The citing note explicitly states it refines prior anchoring research in real-payment decisions, and this cited work is a direct prior source on anchoring and WTP rather than a generic background mention.
